commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because the remaining decision is credential-boundary acceptance, not a concrete automated repair.; Needs attention: No supply-chain issue was found, but the PR intentionally changes a credential-bearing Gateway response and needs maintainer confirmation of the Talk secret-read boundary. (sha=c89fd9c9186c75c418b6b817c6726029b85d16f9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Gateway `talk.config` to materialize only the active Talk provider `apiKey` for scoped `includeSecrets` callers while preserving source-shaped provider maps and redacted read-scope responses, with focused Gateway tests.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +235. Total +276 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection shows current main returns early for `includeSecrets:true` before runtime SecretRef resolution, while the iOS parser only accepts a string `apiKey`. I did not run a physical iOS session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because the remaining decision is credential-boundary acceptance, not a concrete automated repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): iOS Talk treats SecretRef-backed API keys as missing
target.body:
﻿Closes #98209
Related: #91020

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where iOS native Talk users could see `Voice API key missing` for ElevenLabs when the Gateway was configured with `talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey` as a SecretRef. The failure happens because scoped `talk.config({ includeSecrets: true })` exposed the source-shaped provider key to native clients, while iOS only treats `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` as configured when it is a string.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Gateway now resolves the active Talk provider `apiKey` only for callers that already passed the `operator.talk.secrets` or admin scope check, and only feeds that materialized value into the runtime-resolved Talk config path. Ordinary read-scope callers still use the existing strip/redact path and do not receive resolved credential material.

The provider map contract stays source-shaped: `talk.providers.<id>.apiKey` remains the authored SecretRef, while `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` is the runtime string for authorized native clients. A prior attempt (#91020) touched the same early-return seam but raised the credential-boundary concern; this version keeps the fix narrow and relies on the documented `operator.talk.secrets` native-read scope.

## User Impact

Users who pair the iOS app with Talk secret scope can use SecretRef-backed ElevenLabs native Talk configuration without copying the provider key into plaintext config or entering a separate local mobile key. Users without Talk secret scope continue to receive redacted config and cannot read the resolved key.

## Evidence

Issue proof:

- #98209 records the Windows/iOS incident: OpenClaw `2026.6.11-beta.2`, iOS paired successfully, ElevenLabs native Talk showed `Voice API key missing`, and the same key became usable only after a plaintext local workaround.
- Source-level before proof on current `main` (`6cb82eaab865`): `resolveTalkResponseFromConfig` returned early for `includeSecrets`, so runtime Talk provider resolution was skipped.
- iOS source proof: `TalkModeGatewayConfigParser` reads `activeConfig["apiKey"]?.stringValue`, so a SecretRef object in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` is treated as missing.

Live Gateway proof on this branch (`15d77f65287e`):

I started the current branch Gateway with a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`/`OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and a fake env SecretRef only, then called `talk.config` over a live Gateway WebSocket using the Gateway client. The command exited 0.

```json
{
  "command": "live gateway run + GatewayClient talk.config RPC",
  "head": "15d77f65287e",
  "config": "temporary OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH with fake env SecretRef only",
  "scopedIncludeSecrets": {
    "providerMapApiKeyShape": "SecretRef",
    "providerMapApiKeyId": "OC_PROOF_ELEVEN_API_KEY",
    "resolvedApiKeyType": "string",
    "resolvedApiKeyRedacted": "<resolved fake env key>",
    "resolvedVoiceId": "voice-proof"
  },
  "readScope": {
    "providerMapApiKeyShape": "SecretRef",
    "providerMapApiKeyId": "__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__",
    "resolvedApiKeyShape": "SecretRef",
    "resolvedApiKeyId": "__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__",
    "leakedFakeKey": false
  }
}
```

This proves the current-head Gateway response has the intended split contract: scoped native clients receive a string under `talk.resolved.config.apiKey`, while unscoped/read callers do not receive the resolved key.

After-fix local test proof:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.talk-config.test.ts --testNamePattern="gateway talk.config"
```

Result: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 20 passed (20)`. This real Gateway WebSocket integration coverage proves `talk.providers.<id>.apiKey` stays SecretRef-shaped, `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` is the resolved env value for scoped `includeSecrets` callers, and ordinary read-scope callers still receive redacted SecretRef fields.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts --testNamePattern="talk.config"
```

Result: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 6 passed | 60 skipped (66)`.

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/talk.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/server.talk-config.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Result: both exited cleanly.

```text
$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'; python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local --engine codex --thinking medium --stream-engine-output
```

Result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`; `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`.

What was not tested: no physical iOS app session against this branch and no live ElevenLabs synthesis request. The proof uses fake credential strings only; no real API key is included in the issue, PR body, tests, or commit.
target.author: ooiuuii

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
